prompt: Inside the Polymarket 'What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in May 2026?' event, plot a dual-axis chart with strike price on the x-axis ($80 LOW plus $90 → $150 HIGH), implied probability on one y-axis, and 24-hour trading volume on the other y-axis — I want to see the strike skew and where traders are actually concentrated.
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
The market for WTI Crude Oil in May 2026 shows a significant divergence between high-probability price targets and where the heaviest trading volume is occurring. 

As of May 2, 2026, the implied probabilities peak at the **$105 strike (92.5%)** and **$110 strike (74.5%)**, indicating high market confidence that oil will reach these levels. However, the most intense trading activity is concentrated in the "tail" outcomes, particularly at the **$150 strike**, which has seen over $539,000 in 24-hour volume despite having only a 5.5% implied probability.

### WTI May 2026: Probability vs. Trading Volume ($80 - $150)

| Strike Price | Implied Probability | 24-Hour Volume |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| ↓ $80 | 27.5% | $119,774.85 |
| ↓ $85 | 41.5% | $7,329.13 |
| ↓ $90 | 68.5% | $7,356.63 |
| ↓ $95 | 78.5% | $10,157.83 |
| ↑ $105 | 92.5% | $30,547.64 |
| ↑ $110 | 74.5% | $294,658.91 |
| ↑ $115 | 57.5% | $5,400.53 |
| ↑ $120 | 43.5% | $363,472.78 |
| ↑ $130 | 23.5% | $276,585.60 |
| ↑ $140 | 12.3% | $254,677.72 |
| ↑ $150 | 5.5% | $539,363.48 |

The provided chart illustrates a "volatility smile" in volume terms. While the "center" of the probability distribution sits between $95 and $110, l...
